mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 1.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 100k signals weakening labor demand, which reduces consumer spending confidence and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of triggered instances despite PAYEMS declines >100k, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal monetary tightening pressures, which raise discount rates and predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of cases where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals manufacturing weakness, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during supply shocks.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.3 million units signals robust demand for construction, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of HOUST spikes >1.3M, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of UNRATE spikes >0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening consumer and construction demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 60%+ of cases where both RSAFS and HOUST decline, or interaction term coefficient < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% during high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.0% YoY) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient < 0.2 in regression models.
